heading: Oyster growers’ damages from dredging operations
label: 1497 Oyster growers’ damages from dredging operations
text: The United States Court of Federal Claims shall have jurisdiction to render judgment upon any claim for damages to oyster growers on private or leased lands or bottoms arising from dredging operations or use of other machinery and equipment in making river and harbor improvements authorized by Act of Congress.
notes: Historical and Revision NotesBased on title 28, U.S.C., 1940 ed., § 250a (, ; , ).Aug. 30, 1935, ch. 831, § 1349 Stat. 1049July 13, 1943, ch. 23157 Stat. 553The proviso at the end of , U.S.C., 1940 ed., is incorporated in .section 250a of title 28section 2501 of this titleWords “river and harbor improvements” were substituted for “such improvements”, in view of ., 103 Ct. Cl. 160, holding that words, “such improvements” were not limited to the specific improvements listed in the 1935 act, but applied to any river and harbor improvements.Dixon v. U.SChanges were made in phraseology.
